title: rottlera subpeltata
intro: Rottlera subpeltata, also known as the jungle tobacco or wild tobacco, is a medicinal plant native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Rottlera subpeltata is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce stress, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma. The plant's medicinal properties can be harnessed through various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
